texto: My dad grew up in Banbridge, Northern Ireland, desperate to get to London. I grew up in London, so I don't know what it's like to yearn for the big city from a small town.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Daniel Radcliffe: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “My dad grew up in Banbridge, Northern Ireland, desperate to get to London. I grew up in London, s…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, city, small pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de small e know.

texto: I don't know what to do with it. I'm very fortunate to have it, and it gives you room to maneuver. But the main thing about having money is it means you don't have to worry about it. And that for me is a lovely thing. It's not for fast cars and hookers.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Daniel Radcliffe sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “I don't know what to do with it. I'm very fortunate to have it, and it gives you room to maneuver…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a negocios, money, me, útil em momentos de me e worry.
